name: MoneyGram inside CVS - #826
description_short: MoneyGram inside CVS #826 is a money transfer and cash services location offering domestic money sending/receiving and bill payment services through CVS pharmacy locations.
description_long: MoneyGram is a global money transfer service that operates through thousands of agent locations, including this CVS pharmacy branch #826 in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania. The company specializes in enabling consumers to send and receive money domestically and internationally through a network of retail partners. MoneyGram offers convenient access to cash transfer services by embedding their operations within everyday retail locations like CVS, making it easy for customers to conduct transactions during routine shopping trips. The service positions itself as a straightforward alternative to traditional banking for customers needing quick money movement, whether for personal transfers or bill payments. This particular location operates through the CVS store locator system, allowing customers to find the service at their nearest pharmacy. MoneyGram's primary value proposition centers on convenience and accessibility—customers can access money transfer services at locations they already visit regularly. The company operates on a fee-based model, charging customers for each transaction based on transfer amount and destination. While MoneyGram provides legitimate money transfer services, consumers should be aware that transaction fees can be substantial compared to digital payment alternatives, and the service is most cost-effective for recipients without bank accounts or in areas with limited digital banking infrastructure.
address: 1001 South St Ste 51 Attn: South St S/C, 1001 South St, Philadelphia, PA 19147

company_info.headquarters: 1001 South St Ste 51 Attn: South St S/C, 1001 South St, Philadelphia, PA 19147

pros: Convenient location inside CVS pharmacy—accessible during regular store hours without separate trip
National network enabling domestic money transfers to multiple locations nationwide
Same-day or next-day money availability depending on transfer type and destination
No bank account required for receivers—serves unbanked and underbanked consumers
Established brand with decades of operating history and international recognition
Additional services including money orders and bill payment options at location
Simple transaction process designed for quick completion
cons: Transaction fees are typically higher than digital payment apps and bank transfers
Limited service hours tied to CVS store operating schedule, not 24/7 availability
International transfer options may have restrictions or longer processing times depending on destination country
No online account or transaction history tracking mentioned on available website
Requires in-person visit to initiate transfers, unlike digital alternatives
best_for: Consumers without bank accounts needing to send/receive domestic money quickly
Individuals sending money to recipients in countries without robust digital banking
Customers preferring in-person transactions for security or comfort reasons
People already shopping at CVS seeking convenient money transfer while running errands
diagnosis: This location is best for unbanked or underbanked consumers in Philadelphia needing quick domestic money transfers or money orders, particularly those who shop at CVS and prefer in-person transactions. Primary caveat: MoneyGram's transaction fees make it significantly more expensive than digital payment apps and bank transfers, so it is most cost-effective for sending money to recipients without bank accounts rather than as a general-purpose money transfer solution.
services: Domestic money transfers within United States
International money transfers to select countries
In-person cash pickup for received transfers
